message.content:
Explore the current Pattern B implementation in the taxonomy patterns feature:

1. Read TaxonomyPatternMapper.java - focus on the mapPatternB() method and how it generates schemas
2. Read TaxonomyInfo.java - understand IndividualMetadata and what data is captured for Pattern B individuals
3. Read SchemaVisitor.java - understand how Pattern B individuals are detected and what metadata is extracted
4. Look at the test ontology (taxonomy-patterns.owl) to see how Pattern B individuals are currently defined

I need to understand:
- What OWL structure triggers Pattern B detection
- What metadata is currently extracted from individuals
- How the OpenAPI schema is currently generated
- Whether object property assertions on individuals are currently considered

Provide code snippets showing the current handling.

content.text: I'll explore the Pattern B implementation in the taxonomy patterns feature by examining the key files you mentioned.
Now let me read these files to understand the Pattern B implementation:

content.content:
1→package com.grl.component.schema; 2→ 3→import java.util.List; 4→import java.util.Map; 5→import java.util.Optional; 6→import javax.annotation.Nullable; 7→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLClass; 8→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLIndividual; 9→import org.semanticweb.owlapi.model.OWLNamedIndividual; 10→ 11→/** 12→ * Holds detected taxonomy information for a property restriction. 13→ * 14→ *

This record captures all the information needed to generate OpenAPI schemas 15→ * for different taxonomy patterns (A, B, C, D). 16→ * 17→ * @param pattern the detected taxonomy pattern 18→ * @param individuals list of named individuals (for patterns A/B with owl:oneOf) 19→ * @param targetClass the target class for class-based taxonomies (patterns C/D) 20→ * @param hasHierarchy whether the taxonomy has a class hierarchy (pattern C) 21→ * @param conceptScheme the SKOS ConceptScheme individual (pattern D) 22→ * @param propertyName the property name this taxonomy is associated with 23→ * @param metadata individual metadata keyed by individual IRI short name (for pattern B) 24→ * @see TaxonomyPattern 25→ */ 26→public record TaxonomyInfo( 27→ TaxonomyPattern pattern, 28→ List individuals, 29→ @Nullable OWLClass targetClass, 30→ boolean hasHierarchy, 31→ @Nullable OWLIndividual conceptScheme, 32→ String propertyName, 33→ Map metadata 34→) { 35→ 36→ /** 37→ * Metadata for a typed individual (Pattern B). 38→ * 39→ * @param codeValue the code value (from codeValue annotation) 40→ * @param displayName the display name (from displayName annotation) 41→ * @param definition the definition (from skos:definition annotation) 42→ * @param domainClass the domain class type of the individual 43→ */ 44→ public record IndividualMetadata( 45→ @Nullable String codeValue, 46→ @Nullable String displayName, 47→ @Nullable String definition, 48→ @Nullable OWLClass domainClass 49→ ) { 50→ public static IndividualMetadata empty() { 51→ return new IndividualMetadata(null, null, null, null); 52→ } 53→ } 54→ 55→ /** 56→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern A (inline enum with untyped individuals). 57→ */ 58→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternA(String propertyName, List individuals) { 59→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 60→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_A, 61→ individuals, 62→ null, 63→ false, 64→ null, 65→ propertyName, 66→ Map.of() 67→ ); 68→ } 69→ 70→ /** 71→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern B (typed individuals with metadata). 72→ */ 73→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternB( 74→ String propertyName, 75→ List individuals, 76→ Map metadata) { 77→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 78→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_B, 79→ individuals, 80→ null, 81→ false, 82→ null, 83→ propertyName, 84→ metadata 85→ ); 86→ } 87→ 88→ /** 89→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern C (class-based taxonomy). 90→ */ 91→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternC( 92→ String propertyName, 93→ OWLClass targetClass, 94→ List individuals, 95→ boolean hasHierarchy) { 96→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 97→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_C, 98→ individuals, 99→ targetClass, 100→ hasHierarchy, 101→ null, 102→ propertyName, 103→ Map.of() 104→ ); 105→ } 106→ 107→ /** 108→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo for Pattern D (SKOS taxonomy). 109→ */ 110→ public static TaxonomyInfo patternD( 111→ String propertyName, 112→ OWLClass targetClass, 113→ List individuals, 114→ OWLIndividual conceptScheme) { 115→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 116→ TaxonomyPattern.PATTERN_D, 117→ individuals, 118→ targetClass, 119→ true, // SKOS hierarchies use skos:broader/narrower 120→ conceptScheme, 121→ propertyName, 122→ Map.of() 123→ ); 124→ } 125→ 126→ /** 127→ * Creates TaxonomyInfo indicating no taxonomy pattern was detected. 128→ */ 129→ public static TaxonomyInfo none(String propertyName) { 130→ return new TaxonomyInfo( 131→ TaxonomyPattern.NONE, 132→ List.of(), 133→ null, 134→ false, 135→ null, 136→ propertyName, 137→ Map.of() 138→ ); 139→ } 140→ 141→ /** 142→ * @return true if a valid taxonomy pattern was detected 143→ */ 144→ public boolean isTaxonomy() { 145→ return pattern != TaxonomyPattern.NONE; 146→ } 147→ 148→ /** 149→ * @return the target class if present 150→ */ 151→ public Optional getTargetClass() { 152→ return Optional.ofNullable(targetClass); 153→ } 154→ 155→ /** 156→ * @return the concept scheme if present (Pattern D) 157→ */ 158→ public Optional getConceptScheme() { 159→ return Optional.ofNullable(conceptScheme); 160→ } 161→} 162→ Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1→<?xml version="1.0"?>
     2→<rdf:RDF xmlns="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/"
     3→     xml:base="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/"
     4→     xmlns:owl="http://www.w3.org/2002/07/owl#"
     5→     xmlns:rdf="http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#"
     6→     xmlns:xml="http://www.w3.org/XML/1998/namespace"
     7→     xmlns:xsd="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#"
     8→     xmlns:rdfs="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#"
     9→     xmlns:skos="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#"
    10→     xmlns:base="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/"
    11→     xmlns:api="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/"
    12→     xmlns:tp="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/">
    13→
    14→    <owl:Ontology rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/">
    15→        <owl:imports rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/V1.0.2"/>
    16→        <rdfs:comment>Test ontology for taxonomy pattern detection (Patterns A, B, C, D).</rdfs:comment>
    17→    </owl:Ontology>
    18→
    19→    <!-- APIRoot links to API specification -->
    20→    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/APIRoot">
    21→        <rdfs:subClassOf>
    22→            <owl:Restriction>
    23→                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasAPI"/>
    24→                <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0"/>
    25→            </owl:Restriction>
    26→        </rdfs:subClassOf>
    27→    </rdf:Description>
    28→
    29→    <!-- API Specification Class -->
    30→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0">
    31→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/GRLAPISpecifications"/>
    32→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource"/>
    33→        <skos:prefLabel>Taxonomy Patterns API V1.0</skos:prefLabel>
    34→        <api:apiDisplayLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiDisplayLabel>
    35→    </owl:Class>
    36→
    37→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource">
    38→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/hasResourceSchema"/>
    39→        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
    40→        <owl:onClass rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
    41→    </owl:Restriction>
    42→    <owl:Axiom>
    43→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0"/>
    44→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
    45→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasPatternTestResource"/>
    46→        <api:apitag>PatternTestResource</api:apitag>
    47→    </owl:Axiom>
    48→
    49→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    50→    <!-- PATTERN A: Inline enumeration with untyped individuals             -->
    51→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    52→
    53→    <!-- Pattern A - Untyped individuals (only owl:NamedIndividual) -->
    54→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Active">
    55→        <rdfs:label>Active</rdfs:label>
    56→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
    57→
    58→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Inactive">
    59→        <rdfs:label>Inactive</rdfs:label>
    60→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
    61→
    62→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Pending">
    63→        <rdfs:label>Pending</rdfs:label>
    64→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
    65→
    66→    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasStatusA">
    67→        <skos:prefLabel>Has Status A</skos:prefLabel>
    68→        <skos:definition>Pattern A: Status using untyped individuals.</skos:definition>
    69→    </owl:ObjectProperty>
    70→
    71→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    72→    <!-- PATTERN B: Typed individuals with metadata                         -->
    73→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
    74→
    75→    <!-- Pattern B - Domain class for typed individuals -->
    76→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode">
    77→        <skos:prefLabel>Sensor Type Code</skos:prefLabel>
    78→        <skos:definition>Code values for sensor types.</skos:definition>
    79→    </owl:Class>
    80→
    81→    <!-- Pattern B - Typed individuals with codeValue -->
    82→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeAnalog">
    83→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
    84→        <skos:prefLabel>Analog</skos:prefLabel>
    85→        <skos:definition>Analog sensor type.</skos:definition>
    86→        <tp:codeValue>analog</tp:codeValue>
    87→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
    88→
    89→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeDigital">
    90→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
    91→        <skos:prefLabel>Digital</skos:prefLabel>
    92→        <skos:definition>Digital sensor type.</skos:definition>
    93→        <tp:codeValue>digital</tp:codeValue>
    94→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
    95→
    96→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeHybrid">
    97→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeCode"/>
    98→        <skos:prefLabel>Hybrid</skos:prefLabel>
    99→        <skos:definition>Hybrid sensor type.</skos:definition>
   100→        <tp:codeValue>hybrid</tp:codeValue>
   101→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   102→
   103→    <owl:AnnotationProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/codeValue"/>
   104→
   105→    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasSensorType">
   106→        <skos:prefLabel>Has Sensor Type</skos:prefLabel>
   107→        <skos:definition>Pattern B: Sensor type using typed individuals.</skos:definition>
   108→    </owl:ObjectProperty>
   109→
   110→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   111→    <!-- PATTERN C: Class-based taxonomy (extensible)                       -->
   112→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   113→
   114→    <!-- Pattern C - Taxonomy class -->
   115→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode">
   116→        <skos:prefLabel>Environment Code</skos:prefLabel>
   117→        <skos:definition>Operating environment classification.</skos:definition>
   118→    </owl:Class>
   119→
   120→    <!-- Pattern C - Subclasses for hierarchy -->
   121→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment">
   122→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
   123→        <skos:prefLabel>Indoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
   124→    </owl:Class>
   125→
   126→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/OutdoorEnvironment">
   127→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
   128→        <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor Environment</skos:prefLabel>
   129→    </owl:Class>
   130→
   131→    <!-- Pattern C - Individuals -->
   132→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvOffice">
   133→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment"/>
   134→        <skos:prefLabel>Office</skos:prefLabel>
   135→        <tp:codeValue>office</tp:codeValue>
   136→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   137→
   138→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvWarehouse">
   139→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/IndoorEnvironment"/>
   140→        <skos:prefLabel>Warehouse</skos:prefLabel>
   141→        <tp:codeValue>warehouse</tp:codeValue>
   142→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   143→
   144→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvOutdoor">
   145→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/OutdoorEnvironment"/>
   146→        <skos:prefLabel>Outdoor</skos:prefLabel>
   147→        <tp:codeValue>outdoor</tp:codeValue>
   148→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   149→
   150→    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasEnvironment">
   151→        <skos:prefLabel>Has Environment</skos:prefLabel>
   152→        <skos:definition>Pattern C: Environment using class-based taxonomy.</skos:definition>
   153→    </owl:ObjectProperty>
   154→
   155→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   156→    <!-- PATTERN D: SKOS taxonomy                                           -->
   157→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   158→
   159→    <!-- Pattern D - SKOS ConceptScheme -->
   160→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme">
   161→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#ConceptScheme"/>
   162→        <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code System</skos:prefLabel>
   163→        <skos:definition>Code system for measurement context types.</skos:definition>
   164→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   165→
   166→    <!-- Pattern D - Bridge class linking to SKOS scheme -->
   167→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode">
   168→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   169→        <owl:equivalentClass>
   170→            <owl:Restriction>
   171→                <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#inScheme"/>
   172→                <owl:hasValue rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   173→            </owl:Restriction>
   174→        </owl:equivalentClass>
   175→        <skos:prefLabel>Measurement Context Code</skos:prefLabel>
   176→        <skos:definition>SKOS-based measurement context codes.</skos:definition>
   177→    </owl:Class>
   178→
   179→    <!-- Pattern D - SKOS Concepts -->
   180→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextContinuous">
   181→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   182→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   183→        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   184→        <skos:notation>continuous</skos:notation>
   185→        <skos:prefLabel>Continuous</skos:prefLabel>
   186→        <skos:definition>Continuous measurement context.</skos:definition>
   187→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   188→
   189→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextDiscrete">
   190→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   191→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   192→        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   193→        <skos:notation>discrete</skos:notation>
   194→        <skos:prefLabel>Discrete</skos:prefLabel>
   195→        <skos:definition>Discrete measurement context.</skos:definition>
   196→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   197→
   198→    <owl:NamedIndividual rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextTriggered">
   199→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2004/02/skos/core#Concept"/>
   200→        <rdf:type rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   201→        <skos:inScheme rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextScheme"/>
   202→        <skos:notation>triggered</skos:notation>
   203→        <skos:prefLabel>Triggered</skos:prefLabel>
   204→        <skos:definition>Event-triggered measurement context.</skos:definition>
   205→        <skos:broader rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/ContextDiscrete"/>
   206→    </owl:NamedIndividual>
   207→
   208→    <owl:ObjectProperty rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasMeasurementContext">
   209→        <skos:prefLabel>Has Measurement Context</skos:prefLabel>
   210→        <skos:definition>Pattern D: Measurement context using SKOS taxonomy.</skos:definition>
   211→    </owl:ObjectProperty>
   212→
   213→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   214→    <!-- MAIN RESOURCE CLASS                                                -->
   215→    <!-- ================================================================== -->
   216→
   217→    <owl:Class rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource">
   218→        <skos:prefLabel>Pattern Test Resource</skos:prefLabel>
   219→        <skos:definition>Resource class to test all taxonomy patterns.</skos:definition>
   220→
   221→        <!-- apiID -->
   222→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="patternApiId"/>
   223→
   224→        <!-- Pattern A: owl:oneOf with untyped individuals -->
   225→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>
   226→
   227→        <!-- Pattern B: owl:oneOf with typed individuals -->
   228→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>
   229→
   230→        <!-- Pattern C: someValuesFrom named class (extensible) -->
   231→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>
   232→
   233→        <!-- Pattern D: someValuesFrom SKOS-based class -->
   234→        <rdfs:subClassOf rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
   235→    </owl:Class>
   236→
   237→    <!-- apiID restriction -->
   238→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="patternApiId">
   239→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://www.graphresearchlabs.com/base/apiID"/>
   240→        <owl:qualifiedCardinality rdf:datatype="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#nonNegativeInteger">1</owl:qualifiedCardinality>
   241→        <owl:onDataRange rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#string"/>
   242→    </owl:Restriction>
   243→    <owl:Axiom>
   244→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   245→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   246→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="patternApiId"/>
   247→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   248→    </owl:Axiom>
   249→
   250→    <!-- Pattern A: hasStatusA restriction with owl:oneOf (untyped individuals) -->
   251→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr">
   252→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasStatusA"/>
   253→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   254→            <owl:Class>
   255→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   256→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Active"/>
   257→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Inactive"/>
   258→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/Pending"/>
   259→                </owl:oneOf>
   260→            </owl:Class>
   261→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   262→    </owl:Restriction>
   263→    <owl:Axiom>
   264→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   265→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   266→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasStatusARestr"/>
   267→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   268→    </owl:Axiom>
   269→
   270→    <!-- Pattern B: hasSensorType restriction with owl:oneOf (typed individuals) -->
   271→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr">
   272→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasSensorType"/>
   273→        <owl:someValuesFrom>
   274→            <owl:Class>
   275→                <owl:oneOf rdf:parseType="Collection">
   276→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeAnalog"/>
   277→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeDigital"/>
   278→                    <rdf:Description rdf:about="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/SensorTypeHybrid"/>
   279→                </owl:oneOf>
   280→            </owl:Class>
   281→        </owl:someValuesFrom>
   282→    </owl:Restriction>
   283→    <owl:Axiom>
   284→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   285→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   286→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasSensorTypeRestr"/>
   287→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   288→    </owl:Axiom>
   289→
   290→    <!-- Pattern C: hasEnvironment restriction (someValuesFrom named class) -->
   291→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr">
   292→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasEnvironment"/>
   293→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/EnvironmentCode"/>
   294→    </owl:Restriction>
   295→    <owl:Axiom>
   296→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   297→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   298→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasEnvironmentRestr"/>
   299→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   300→    </owl:Axiom>
   301→
   302→    <!-- Pattern D: hasMeasurementContext restriction (someValuesFrom SKOS class) -->
   303→    <owl:Restriction rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr">
   304→        <owl:onProperty rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/hasMeasurementContext"/>
   305→        <owl:someValuesFrom rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/MeasurementContextCode"/>
   306→    </owl:Restriction>
   307→    <owl:Axiom>
   308→        <owl:annotatedSource rdf:resource="https://example.com/taxonomy-patterns/PatternTestResource"/>
   309→        <owl:annotatedProperty rdf:resource="http://www.w3.org/2000/01/rdf-schema#subClassOf"/>
   310→        <owl:annotatedTarget rdf:nodeID="hasMeasurementContextRestr"/>
   311→        <api:apiLabel>TaxonomyPatternsAPIV1.0</api:apiLabel>
   312→    </owl:Axiom>
   313→
   314→</rdf:RDF>
   315→

content.text: Perfect! Now let me search for any test files related to Pattern B to understand better how it's currently tested:

Perfect! Now let me look for the OntologyConstants to see what constants are available:
